name: Observational & Epidemiological Studies
description: Epidemiological studies, patient registries, natural history studies, and surveys describing CH patterns in populations.

all_trials.title: China Headache and Vertigo Registry Study
all_trials.sponsor: Second Affiliated Hospital, School of Medicine, Zhejiang University
all_trials.what_tested: A massive long-term registry collecting clinical data, biomarkers, imaging, genetics, and treatment outcomes for headache and vertigo patients in China.
all_trials.key_result: Recruiting, with 10,000 participants enrolled. Runs until 2050.
all_trials.patient_relevance: This huge database may eventually reveal patterns about CH that smaller studies miss, but it is a data collection effort, not testing a treatment.

all_trials.title: The Will Erwin Headache Research Center - Cluster Headache Study
all_trials.sponsor: The University of Texas Health Science Center, Houston
all_trials.what_tested: A long-term observational research network collecting data on cluster headache and trigeminal neuralgia patients at the Will Erwin Headache Research Center.
all_trials.patient_relevance: This US-based research network is building the largest CH patient database -- it supports multiple sub-studies and could accelerate treatment discoveries.

all_trials.title: Cluster Headache Treatment Survey
all_trials.sponsor: The University of Texas Health Science Center, Houston
all_trials.what_tested: A large online survey asking CH patients about treatment effectiveness and their views on various treatments.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 2,487 participants -- one of the largest CH surveys ever conducted.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Patient-reported data on what actually works in real life -- this survey captures the CH community's experience with treatments, complementing clinical trial data.

all_trials.title: An Open Internet-based Survey and Natural Language Processing Project Analysing Written Monologues by Headache Patients
all_trials.what_tested: Using natural language processing (AI) to analyze how headache patients describe their condition in writing, to potentially improve diagnosis.
all_trials.patient_relevance: AI analysis of patient descriptions could someday help diagnose CH faster -- the average CH patient waits years for a correct diagnosis.

all_trials.title: Sleep, Circadian Rhythms and Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: Detailed study of sleep patterns and circadian (24-hour body clock) rhythms in episodic cluster headache patients, comparing in-bout versus out-of-bout phases.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 8 participants. Very small but detailed physiological study.
all_trials.patient_relevance: CH attacks follow circadian patterns -- understanding the sleep and body-clock changes could lead to chronotherapy (timing-based treatment) approaches.

all_trials.title: Efficacy of Modified Atkins Ketogenic Diet in Chronic Cluster Headache: a Prospective Case Series
all_trials.what_tested: Modified Atkins ketogenic diet (very low carbohydrate) as a treatment for chronic cluster headache, based on its success in epilepsy and preliminary migraine data.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 18 participants. No formal results posted, but published research from this group showed encouraging reductions in attack frequency.
all_trials.patient_relevance: The ketogenic diet is something patients can try on their own -- early signals suggest it may help some CH patients, possibly through anti-inflammatory effects.

all_trials.title: Effect of Breathing Techniques on Migraine Attacks and Severity
all_trials.what_tested: Whether breathing techniques can reduce migraine attack frequency and severity when added to standard medication.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 86 participants. This was a migraine study, not a cluster headache study.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Not directly relevant to CH -- breathing techniques were tested for migraine only.
all_trials.dose_tested: Breathing techniques as add-on to migraine medication

all_trials.title: Context-awareness, Physiological Monitoring, and Machine Learning in Migraine and Cluster Headache
all_trials.what_tested: Using wearable sensors and machine learning to detect stress, behavior changes, and predict headache attacks in migraine and cluster headache patients.
all_trials.key_result: Completed with 19 participants. Pilot study for wearable-based headache prediction.
all_trials.patient_relevance: If a smartwatch could predict an incoming CH attack, patients could treat preemptively -- this pilot explored whether that is technically feasible.

all_trials.title: A Prospective Observational Registry of Primary Headache Patients Treated With Ausanil
all_trials.what_tested: Ausanil (an intranasal product) for primary headache disorders including cluster headache -- an observational safety and tolerability registry.
all_trials.key_result: Terminated with 20 participants. No results posted.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Ausanil was a commercial nasal product marketed for headache relief -- this registry was terminated and the treatment has not gained traction.

all_trials.title: Quest to Analyze One Thousand Humans Meditating
all_trials.what_tested: Studying molecular, physiological, and emotional effects of intensive meditation in a large group including both healthy people and chronic pain patients.
all_trials.key_result: Enrolling by invitation with 3,000 participants. Not specific to CH.
all_trials.patient_relevance: Meditation may have general benefits for chronic pain sufferers, but this is not a CH-specific study.
